mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes subproject that provides declarative APIs and tooling for managing the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies provisioning, upgrading, and operating multiple clusters across various infrastructure environments.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
To install Cluster API, first ensure you have the required tools installed. Then, apply the necessary CRDs and components using the following commands:

```bash
# Install Cluster API components for v1.9
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.9.0/cluster-api-components.yaml
```

**Note:** Replace `v1.9.0` with the desired version if necessary.

steps.title: Bootstrap the management cluster
steps.description:
Set up a management cluster using the following command:

```bash
# Create a management cluster using kind
kind create cluster --name capi-management
```

**Note:** Ensure you have `kind` installed. You can also use other methods like `kubeadm` if preferred.

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cluster API components are running correctly:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n capi-system
```

You should see the Cluster API controllers running.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure RBAC for your Cluster API installation:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.9.0/rbac.yaml
```

This will set up necessary permissions for the Cluster API components.

uninstall.title: Remove Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Cluster API, delete the components and CRDs:

```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.9.0/cluster-api-components.yaml
kubectl delete crd clusters.cluster.x-k8s.io machines.cluster.x-k8s.io
```

Make sure to clean up any resources created by Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API to v1.9
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster state. Then, upgrade by applying the new components:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.9.0/cluster-api-components.yaml
```

**Pre-upgrade check:** Ensure all components are running and healthy. If issues arise, you can roll back by reapplying the previous version's components.

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If Cluster API pods are not starting, check the logs:

```bash
kubectl logs -n capi-system <pod-name>
```

Look for errors related to configuration or missing permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Management cluster not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the management cluster is unresponsive, verify the cluster status:

```bash
kubectl cluster-info
```

Ensure that the control plane components are healthy.

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors regarding missing CRDs, ensure they are applied:

```bash
kubectl get crd
```

If they are missing, reapply the CRDs from the installation YAML.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced improved error handling and additional metrics for monitoring.
versionNotes.deprecations: The old v1.7 API endpoints are deprecated and will be removed in future releases.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and enhanced documentation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Some legacy features have been marked for deprecation.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Cluster API will have all components running in the `capi-system` namespace, with the management cluster responding to commands. You should be able to create and manage Kubernetes clusters declaratively.
codeSnippets:
# Install Cluster API components for v1.9
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.9.0/cluster-api-components.yaml

# Create a management cluster using kind
kind create cluster --name capi-management

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl and kind installed for managing Kubernetes clusters.
